Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses [number of ounces per week] ounces per week. A leap year has 366 days, so she washes her hair [number of times per year] times throughout the year. To find the total amount of shampoo she needs, we multiply the amount used per wash by the number of washes in a leap year. This gives us [total ounces needed], which tells us how many ounces of shampoo she requires for the entire year.

Since shampoo is sold in 14-ounce bottles, we can divide the total ounces needed by 14 to determine how many bottles she will need. If the result is not a whole number, she will need to round up to the next whole number to ensure she has enough shampoo for the entire year. Therefore, Brittany will need [number of bottles] bottles of shampoo to last through a leap year.
